title: torilis scabra
intro: Torilis scabra, also known as hairy foxglove or beard-tooth, is a biennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant's leaves and roots have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in alleviating symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Torilis scabra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve symptoms of colds, fever, and urinary tract infections, and to promote digestive health.
